Home / Weight Loss

Weight Loss Calculators & Guides (New Zealand)

Welcome to our Weight Loss hub. Here you will find scientific, dietitian-reviewed weight loss percentage calculators, milestone planners, and tracking tools to help you monitor your journey safely and successfully. Tracking weight loss as a percentage of body weight, rather than just raw scale weight, is the preferred clinical approach to measuring metabolic change and setting realistic health goals.

Core Weight Loss Tools

  • Weight Loss Percentage Calculator - Track weight loss as a percentage of initial body mass.
  • GLP-1 Weight Loss Calculator - Estimate weight loss trajectory with Wegovy, Ozempic, or Mounjaro.
  • Postpartum & Breastfeeding Calculator - Set safe, breastfeeding-friendly fat loss deficits.
  • Bariatric Surgery Weight Loss Calculator - Forecast excess weight loss post-bypass/VSG.
  • Dog Weight Loss Calculator - Manage canine nutrition, calories, and feeding portions.
  • Newborn Weight Loss Calculator - Track infant weight loss percentage after birth.
  • Infant Weight Loss Calculator - Pediatric tracking and growth recovery tool.
  • Baby Weight Loss Calculator - Standardized pediatric growth patterns chart.
  • Biggest Loser Calculator - Calculate weight loss percentage using the TV show formula.
  • Fat Loss Percentage Calculator - Separate muscle loss from pure fat burn.

Evidence-Based Guides

  • How to Calculate Weight Loss Percentage: Step-by-Step Formula
  • The Science of 5% Weight Loss: Why It's Your First Major Health Milestone
  • Fat Loss vs. Weight Loss: How to Keep Muscle and Shed Fat
  • Monthly Weight Loss Percentage Chart & Progress Tracker
  • GLP-1 Medications for Weight Loss: Trajectories, Costs, and Clinical Guides
  • Safe Postpartum Weight Loss: A Guide for Breastfeeding Mothers
  • Bariatric Surgery Weight Loss Trajectory & Nutrition Guidelines

Scientific Citation & Outbound Authority: Weight loss management is thoroughly investigated by global health entities. Learn more about the clinical biology of adipose tissue reduction at Wikipedia's Weight Loss Article.
